solution.p: We will factor 64x^{2}t^{2}-81 where a=64, b=0, and c=-81.

Difference of Squares Formula: The formula for a difference of squares is a^2-b^2 = (a+b)(a-b) Note that the polynomial 64x^{2}t^{2}-81 matches the formula's right side, and we have: \begin{aligned}[t]64x^{2}t^{2}-81\amp =\left(8xt\right)^2-\left(9\right)^2\newline \amp =\left(8xt+9\right)\mathopen{}\left(8xt-9\right)\end{aligned}
AC Method: In this method, we first calculate the product of a and c in ax^2+bx+c. For this problem, we have (64) \cdot (-81) = -5184.

The number -5184 can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways:  egin{array}{lll}1(-5184)\amp -1(5184)\amp 2(-2592)\amp -2(2592)
ewline3(-1728)\amp -3(1728)\amp 4(-1296)\amp -4(1296)
ewline6(-864)\amp -6(864)\amp 8(-648)\amp -8(648)
ewline9(-576)\amp -9(576)\amp 12(-432)\amp -12(432)
ewline16(-324)\amp -16(324)\amp 18(-288)\amp -18(288)
ewline24(-216)\amp -24(216)\amp 27(-192)\amp -27(192)
ewline32(-162)\amp -32(162)\amp 36(-144)\amp -36(144)
ewline48(-108)\amp -48(108)\amp 54(-96)\amp -54(96)
ewline64(-81)\amp -64(81)\amp 72(-72)\amp -72(72)
ewline\end{array} Looking at the corresponding sums, we want a sum of 0, and this sum happens with (-72)+72=0 So we write  egin{aligned}[t]64x^{2}t^{2}-81\amp =64x^{2}t^{2}-72xt+72xt-81
ewline \amp =8xt\mathopen{}\left(8xt-9ight)+9\mathopen{}\left(8xt-9ight)
ewline \amp =\left(8xt+9ight)\mathopen{}\left(8xt-9ight)\end{aligned}Generic Rectangles Method: First, we set up generic rectangles by putting the first term and third term in 64x^{2}t^{2}-81 into the top left and bottom right rectangles:

The first term's coefficient, 64, can be factored into the product of two numbers (where the first factor is positive) in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(64)\amp 2(32)\amp 4(16)\amp 8(8)\newline\end{array}
The third term, -81, can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(-81)\amp -1(81)\amp 3(-27)\amp -3(27)\newline9(-9)\amp -9(9)\amp \end{array}

We put each pair into the corresponding places next to those generic rectangles, and try to match the area of those rectangles with 64x^{2}t^{2}-81 by guess-and-check. The following generic rectangles show the solution:
Dimensions of those generic rectangles in the diagram give us the solution: \begin{aligned}[t]64x^{2}t^{2}-81\amp =\left(8xt+9\right)\mathopen{}\left(8xt-9\right)\end{aligned}
